name: CmdStanPy (Python Interface to Stan)

performance.notes: C++ Stan sampler; NUTS HMC with dynamic tree depth; parallel chains

agent_readiness.notes: Python interface to Stan probabilistic programming language. import cmdstanpy. cmdstanpy.install_cmdstan(). sm = cmdstanpy.CmdStanModel(stan_file='model.stan'). fit = sm.sample(data={'N': 100, 'y': y_data}, chains=4, iter_sampling=1000). fit.summary(). fit.draws_pd() → pandas DataFrame. fit.stan_variable('mu') → np.array. Optimize: sm.optimize(). Variational: sm.variational(). Generate quantities: sm.generate_quantities(). Stan language: data block, parameters block, model block. Use arviz for diagnostics: az.summary(fit), az.plot_trace(fit).

gotchas: Stan model must be written in Stan language (separate .stan files) — not Python
CmdStan must be installed separately: cmdstanpy.install_cmdstan()
NUTS HMC requires gradient computation — Stan auto-differentiates but model must be continuous
Discrete parameters not supported by HMC — must marginalize
Divergences and R-hat diagnostics require arviz for proper analysis
best_when: Full Bayesian inference with gold-standard NUTS HMC sampler
When Stan's expressive language is preferred over Python-native PPLs
Academic/research Bayesian statistics requiring exact HMC diagnostics
avoid_when: Deep learning integration (use Pyro or NumPyro)
Quick exploratory Bayesian analysis (use PyMC for easier setup)
Discrete latent variables (marginalize or use different PPL)
summary: CmdStanPy interfaces Python with Stan's gold-standard NUTS HMC sampler. CmdStanModel(stan_file='model.stan').sample(data={...}, chains=4). fit.draws_pd() → DataFrame. fit.stan_variable(). Optimize, variational inference. Integrate arviz for diagnostics. BSD-3 by Stan Dev Team. Best for research-grade full Bayesian inference. Requires Stan language knowledge and separate CmdStan install. Discrete params need marginalization. No deep learning integration.
